reasoning_content: We need to check if 22733 is a prime number. A quick sanity check: check divisibility by small primes. First, note last digit is 3, so not divisible by 2 or 5. Sum of digits: 2+2+7+3+3=17, not divisible by 3, so not divisible by 3. Check 7: 22733/7? 7*3247=22729, remainder 4, so not divisible. 11: alternating sum: (2-2+7-3+3)=7, not multiple of 11. 13: 13*1748=22724, remainder 9, so no. 17: 17*1337=22729, remainder 4. 19: 19*1196=22724, remainder 9. 23: 23*988=22724, remainder 9. 29: 29*784=22736, too high; 29*783=22707, remainder 26. 31: 31*733=22723, remainder 10. 37: 37*614=22718, remainder 15. 41: 41*554=22714, remainder 19. 43: 43*529=22747, too high; 43*528=22704, remainder 29. 47: 47*484=22748, too high; 47*483=22701, remainder 32. 53: 53*429=22737, too high; 53*428=22684, remainder 49. 59: 59*385=22715, remainder 18. 61: 61*373=22753, too high; 61*372=22692, remainder 41. 67: 67*339=22713, remainder 20. 71: 71*320=22720, remainder 13. 73: 73*311=22703, remainder 30. 79: 79*288=22752, too high; 79*287=22673, remainder 60. 83: 83*274=22742, too high; 83*273=22659, remainder 74. 89: 89*255=22695, remainder 38. 97: 97*234=22698, remainder 35. Next prime 101: 101*225=22725, remainder 8. 103: 103*221=22763, too high; 103*220=22660, remainder 73. 107: 107*212=22684, remainder 49. 109: 109*209=22781, too high; 109*208=22672, remainder 61. 113: 113*201=22713, remainder 20. 127: 127*179=22733? Let's check: 127*179 = 127*180 - 127 = 22860 - 127 = 22733. Yes! So 22733 = 127 * 179. Hence not prime. Sanity check: both factors are primes? 127 is prime, 179 is prime? 179 mod 13? 13*13=169, remainder 10; 179 mod 17=179-170=9; mod 11=3+? Actually 11*16=176 remainder 3; mod 7=7*25=175 remainder 4; mod 5 no; mod 3 sum digits 1+7+9=17 not divisible. So 179 is likely prime. So composite. Answer: not prime.
